# Termination notice template — vacant land due diligence

Purpose: attorney-review draft for terminating seller purchase contract during due-diligence / feasibility period.

> Not legal advice. Confirm exact notice requirements, delivery method, and timing with Florida counsel and the closing/title company.

## Email subject

```text
Termination Notice — [Property / Parcel ID] — Purchase Contract dated [DATE]
```

## Email body

```text
Seller: [SELLER NAME]
Property: [PROPERTY ADDRESS / PARCEL ID]
Purchase Contract effective date: [DATE]

Dear [SELLER NAME],

Buyer hereby gives written notice that Buyer is terminating the Purchase Contract for the above-referenced Property during the Due Diligence / Feasibility Period.

This termination is made pursuant to Buyer’s rights under the Purchase Contract and any applicable addenda. Buyer will not proceed to closing.

Please direct the Closing Agent / Escrow Agent to cancel the file and release any deposit according to the Purchase Contract and escrow instructions.

Regards,

[BUYER NAME]
[BUYER ENTITY]
[PHONE]
[EMAIL]
```

## Delivery checklist

- [ ] Confirm termination deadline has not passed.
- [ ] Send to seller email listed in contract.
- [ ] Send to title/closing agent.
- [ ] Send by any additional required method in the base contract.
- [ ] Save timestamped copy in deal file.
- [ ] Update Land Dealflow OS lead status: `terminated_due_diligence`.
- [ ] Record reason: wetlands / slope / access / title / builder rejected / price / other.

## Attorney questions

1. Is email notice sufficient under the chosen Florida contract?
2. Should notice include reason, or remain reason-neutral?
3. Is seller signature required to release deposit if deposit exists?
4. What exact language should be used to preserve rights and avoid accidental admission/default?
